title: Does Anno veiw Directors Cut as a seprate work
display_title: Does Anno veiw Directors Cut as a seprate work
wikitext:
[]: Does Anno veiw Director's Cut as a seprate work?             Quote Post by Number 44 » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:02 pm A thought popped into my head not too long ago, over the deliberate continuity errors in episode 24, and some of the other content, namely Rei's death/unit 00 transformation, in directors cut episodes. I finally have come to the only logical conclusion, and the accepted one for these forums apparently, that elements like the 15 monolith's are on purpose. But that leaves the question why? The directors cut is obviously altered to match up better with End of Evangelion. Rei's death/unit 00 transformation, the Eva graveyard, , and Kaworu having Adam's salvaged soul, therefore being a 1-for-1 counter part to Rei. I could go on. It's been stated that EoE is direct a replacement for the series orginal two final episodes. If that's so then the directors cut episodes would also be replacements for the orginal TV episodes. My question is, could it be that the series creators view the original cut of the series and it's original ending, as a separate work from directors cut and EoE? Or is it really what it appears to be a revisionist's work, doing what could not be done in the orginal time/budget restraints? Before you dissmiss this theory due to my poor communication skills at least consider the possiblities.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:10 pm I might be mistaken, but I'm pretty sure that the Japanese DVD releases of NGE don't even include the original on-air versions of the episodes, and the director's cut is simply known as the "video version" there. If that's the case, I think it says a lot towards whether Anno thinks they're an improved version of the same story. Someone correct me if I'm wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:26 pm ^ You mean we would have stuff in a Japanese anime that the Japanese don't even have? That would be rare. And pretty cool. But to answer the question: Maybe. Anno probably did see the Director's Cuts/Video Editions as works that are separate from the Originally Aired Editions, or at least as one work that separates into two different endings. That is, only if one supports the idea that EoE did actually reach a different conclusion than EoTV. Just as an example, if you cut out the extended scenes in episode 23, you completely lose its visual symmetry with End of Evangelion . Whereas if you just watch the the Originally Aired Editions, it all wraps up neatly into it's own conclusion without referencing too much of EoE. (That is, if you think that the still frames of Misato's and Ritsuko's dead bodies in EoTV don't need to be expounded upon.)

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Aug 10, 2010 4:37 pm esselfortium wrote: I might be mistaken, but I'm pretty sure that the Japanese DVD releases of NGE don't even include the original on-air versions of the episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Aug 10, 2010 11:10 pm What ath said is correct.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenithfleet » Thu Aug 12, 2010 7:22 am Ath and SailorStarDust have already answered this question, but just to throw in an anecdote, I was in Japan back in 2001 when most things were still on VHS, and only found the DC episodes for sale/rental. I was renting and watching them for a bit of Japanese practice (knowing the dub off by heart you see...), and the start of Epsiode 21 shocked the proverbial out of me. I'd never even heard of the director's cuts before then. Meanwhile nobody (based on my scientifically selected sample of random school friends) seemed to know what I was talking about when I tried to convince them there was a version of events in which Unit 00 just blew up instead of all that Claymation Angel Carrot business. FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Just as an example, if you cut out the extended scenes in episode 23, you completely lose its visual symmetry with End of Evangelion. Whereas if you just watch the the Originally Aired Editions, it all wraps up neatly into it's own conclusion without referencing too much of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Aug 12, 2010 1:34 pm Zenithfleet wrote: It's worth noting that EoTV 25 and 26 were definitely available on Japanese VHS back then (I could be wrong, but I think they were arranged as 25 + 25' on one tape and 26 + 26' on the other). They weren't erased from the record the way the OA 21-24 were. So I think Gainax believes the DC eps are genuine replacements for 21-24 that work for both EoTV and EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 12, 2010 2:42 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Anno probably did see the Director's Cuts/Video Editions as works that are separate from the Originally Aired Editions, or at least as one work that separates into two different endings. That is, only if one supports the idea that EoE did actually reach a different conclusion than EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Aug 13, 2010 3:34 pm ^ Well, there you have it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Aug 13, 2010 5:14 pm NemZ wrote: Yes, that's exactly how it is intended. [RCB quote which, by the way, says nothing about the similarity of the two conclusions, just that they are different endings. which they inherently are, by virtue of not being the same work, and doesn't answer any questions about different outcomes at all.]

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 13, 2010 6:40 pm esselfortium wrote: How come supplementary materials only apply when they support your argument? :-\

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 15, 2010 1:01 pm esselfortium wrote: How come supplementary materials only apply when they support your argument? :-\

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Aug 15, 2010 2:12 pm Hilariously, it also states outright that until 24 ends, EoTV and EoE had the exact same continuity. Henceforth, I suggest that all people arguing for anything say STFU whenever anyone says that 21'-24' are invalid for arguing EoTV points. However, since the RCB is not in English last I checked, I refuse to draw any conclusions from it until I have someone show up with the original Japanese, explaining what each symbol means and what each bit of phrasing has the potential TO mean.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 15, 2010 2:45 pm Legendary wrote: Hilariously, it also states outright that until 24 ends, EoTV and EoE had the exact same continuity.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 15, 2010 5:30 pm TehDonutKing wrote: It effectivly confirmed no concurrency.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Aug 15, 2010 6:02 pm NemZ wrote: It doesn't mention the DC episodes at all, which shouldn't be suprising since they didn't exist in any form other than Death when EoE premiered.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 15, 2010 6:59 pm Esselfortium wrote: It said nothing about concurrency. It said there are two endings. That implies nothing whatsoever about the similarity of the events within them.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 15, 2010 7:02 pm NemZ wrote: 'the story branches' seems pretty clearly to say the two aren't heading to the same conclusion. If they aren't going to the same place how can they be concurrent?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 15, 2010 7:12 pm I thought we were discussing what we think Anno and co. think about it, not how we individually interpret things. For the latter point it doesn't matter at all what the liner notes have to say on the matter and I came to my conclusion long before noticing that quote (or joining this forum, for that matter). Edit: Why did I use the word 'matter' so often in this post? What is the matter with me today? Eh, I guess it doesn't really matter as long as it isn't a serious matter.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 15, 2010 7:43 pm NemZ wrote: I thought we were discussing what we think Anno and co. think about it, not how we individually interpret things. For the latter point it doesn't matter at all what the liner notes have to say on the matter and I came to my conclusion long before noticing that quote (or joining this forum, for that matter).
